World Chess PLC (LSE: CHSS) is the official commercial partner of the International Chess Federation (FIDE). latest.title: Proposed Expansion of FIDE Partnership
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 6427H
World Chess PLC
10 June 2026
World Chess Plc
("World Chess" or the "Company" or the "Group")
Proposed Expansion of FIDE Partnership
Term Sheet signed with FIDE Pathway from online play to official FIDE rating.
World Chess PLC (LSE: CHSS), the official commercial partner of the International Chess Federation ("FIDE"), is pleased to announce that it has signed a Term Sheet with FIDE to renew and significantly expand the partnership under which the Group operates FIDE's official online platform, worldchess.com.
For the first time, qualifying online players will be able to convert their online rating into an official FIDE over-the-board ("OTB") rating. This opens the FIDE rating system, historically accessible only through OTB tournaments, to the very large population of serious chess players worldwide who have no realistic OTB pathway available to them, in particular in India, Latin America, Africa and South-East Asia as well as other countries.
Highlights
●
A new commercial framework that makes World Chess the sole operator of the only official route from online play to official FIDE OTB rating.
●
A new revenue line - conversion fees, set by World Chess in consultation with FIDE - layered on top of the Group's existing subscription revenue from worldchess.com.
●
Renewal of the FIDE Online Arena partnership for an initial two-year term, with annual extension options through to August 2031, deepening and extending the Group's exclusive position as FIDE's online rating operator.
●
Annual fee payable by World Chess to FIDE of EUR 500,000 in the initial two-year term, an increase of EUR 100,000 on the current annual fee, plus a revenue share applied on a tiered basis to sales above EUR 2 million per annum, with incremental fee increases in any extension years.
●
A federation incentive of USD 1 per conversion, paid by World Chess to the player's national federation, aligning national federations behind the programme and supporting recruitment of members in markets where federation activity is currently low.
●
Full FIDE regulatory oversight is retained throughout: ratings remain capped, anti-cheating controls are operated jointly with FIDE, and FIDE retains the right to terminate against agreed KPIs.
Why this matters?
The official OTB FIDE rating is the standard credential in for OTB chess players. Of the hundreds of millions of people who play chess seriously online today, only a small minority hold one - not because they aren't strong enough, but because the OTB infrastructure required to earn one does not exist where most of them live. This agreement creates, for the first time, an official route into the FIDE rating system for that significant group of chess players, with World Chess as its sole operator.
The Board expects the framework to expand the Group's addressable market materially, to add a new revenue stream distinct from subscription and advertising revenue, and to strengthen conversion of free users on worldchess.com into paying users by making the commercial offer materially more valuable.
Ilya Merenzon, Chief Executive Officer of World Chess PLC, commented:
"
FIDE and World Chess are calling this the First Rating Experiment, and that is what it is - the first time in the history of the game that an official FIDE rating can be accessible to millions of players. I believe one of the next world champions will come through this route, because this is an enormous source of chess talent that has so far had no way in. For the millions of players around the world who have been playing serious chess online for years with no path into the official system, professional chess is about to open up.
For World Chess, this is a major step, and one we have spent years building towards - investing in the platform, the infrastructure, and in particular the anti-cheating capability that makes a programme of this kind credible in the first place. We are proud to be the company building this route, under direct FIDE supervision, and to be taking part in what we believe is a genuine turning point for the sport.
"
Status and next steps
The Term Sheet is non-binding pending discussion with the community. The Parties are working towards a definitive long-form agreement, targeted for execution in Q2 of 2026. In the meantime, World Chess has agreed that it will work with several chess federations under FIDE supervision to add them as launch partners and get access to a substantial number of players.
Ahead of execution, World Chess and FIDE will conduct a public consultation on the technical parameters of the conversion mechanism. Feedback from that consultation may result in changes to those parameters before signing. The Company will provide updates on material milestones during the consultation and on signature of the definitive agreement.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the UK Market Abuse Regulation. With the publication of this announcement, this information is now considered to be in the public domain.
Enquiries:
World Chess PLC
Ilya Merenzon, Chief Executive Officer merenzon@worldchess.com
AbrR Capital Limited - Financial Adviser
David Coffman / Dan Harris +44 (0) 7469 0930
About World Chess PLC
World Chess PLC (LSE: CHSS) is the official commercial partner of the International Chess Federation (FIDE). The Group operates worldchess.com - the single online platform where players earn ratings and titles recognised by FIDE and compete in official tournaments. World Chess also produces The World Chess Show, a chess broadcast property carried in 150 countries, and designs and sells the official sets and boards of the World Championship. The Company's shares are admitted to the Official List (Standard Listing) and traded on the main market of the London Stock Exchange under the ticker CHSS.
Forward-looking statements
This announcement contains forward-looking statements relating to the proposed new agreement with FIDE, the expected timing and terms of that agreement, and the expected commercial, operational and financial effects of the conversion framework. These statements are subject to risks and uncertainties including, but not limited to, the negotiation and execution of a definitive agreement on terms consistent with the Term Sheet, the outcome of the public consultation, and the operation of the conversion mechanism in practice. Actual outcomes may differ materially. The Company undertakes no obligation to update these statements except as required by applicable law, the UK Listing Rules or the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

latest.title: Launch of Global Brand Campaign
latest.content:
26 May 2026 11:37
RNS Number : 7290F
World Chess PLC
26 May 2026
World Chess Plc
("World Chess" or the "Company")
Launch of Global Brand Campaign
World Chess Plc (LSE: CHSS | FRA: QZ9), the London-listed chess company and official commercial partner of the International Chess Federation (FIDE), is pleased to announce the launch of "Chess Is Very Good For You", an umbrella brand and player-acquisition campaign that has gone live across central London and is being made available to national chess federations worldwide.
Key Highlights
·
A new campaign, "Chess Is Very Good For You", has launched across the London Underground - including Central and Jubilee line sites at Liverpool Street, Tottenham Court Road and Canary Wharf - placing it in the heart of the City of London.
·
The campaign is designed to grow chess as a sport as well as benefit the Company - by promoting the merits of the game itself, it aims to broaden the overall audience World Chess serves.
·
It is built on independent research commissioned by World Chess into the benefits of regular play and is designed to expand over time as the Company publishes further findings - adding new reasons under the same banner.
·
It is intended to acquire new players to the Company's platform, worldchess.com, focusing on the large majority of the chess audience who are newcomers rather than existing competitive players and to deepen engagement with the UK community while building the brand for a global audience.
·
The full creative package is being offered free of charge to FIDE's c.200 national federations to run in their own cities and languages - a distribution model designed to deliver international reach at minimal incremental cost.
·
The campaign marks a deliberate step into growth, expressed through the bold, distinctive design for which World Chess is known, and reinforces the Company's integrated platform, broadcast and live-events model.
Background
The campaign speaks to two audiences at once. It is a public-interest message - research links regular play to better focus, decision-making and long-term cognitive health - and a message to the City it sits in: 600 million people play chess, and the company building for them is headquartered in London and listed as CHSS.
World Chess has built worldchess.com to compete with the category's leading platforms on product. The growth is not only in fighting over already-affiliated players, but in winning the far larger population of newcomers, in Europe, Asia and other growth markets. The campaign scales through distribution, not only paid media. National federations can run the campaign in their own cities and languages, extending the reach.
It is a deliberate move into growth, in the bold design World Chess is known for - and the marketing expression of the Company's integrated model: platform (worldchess.com), broadcast products and live events working as one system to acquire and retain a global audience.
Ilya Merenzon, Chief Executive Officer of World Chess Plc, commented:
"Chess is one of the world's most played games and still one of its least commercialised. This is an umbrella campaign - what's good for chess is good for World Chess, and chess is genuinely good for you. We've commissioned our own research into why, and we'll keep adding reasons. We're doing it with the bold design we're known for, because a move into growth should look like something. The real opportunity is the hundreds of millions of people who play but have not had a company built for them. We intend to be that company."
Further information about the campaign can be found here https://worldchess.com/projects/chess-is-good-for-you
World Chess
Ilya Merenzon, CEO
merenzon@worldchess.com
AlbR Capital Limited - Financial Adviser
David Coffman / Dan Harris
+44 (0) 20 7469 0930
About World Chess
World Chess Plc (LSE: CHSS) is the official commercial partner of FIDE, the International Chess Federation. The Company operates worldchess.com, the official FIDE-rated gaming and subscription platform, with over one million registered users; produces the World Chess Show, broadcast across 50+ markets. World Chess has been traded on the London Stock Exchange since 2023 and is only publicly listed chess company in the world.

latest.title: Directorate Change and Search for Permanent Chair
latest.content:
22 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 3332F
World Chess PLC
22 May 2026
World Chess Plc
("World Chess" or the "Company")
Appointment of Interim Chair and Commencement of Search for Permanent Chair
World Chess (LSE: CHSS), a leading chess organisation that develops and operates worldchess.com, the official online gaming platform of the International Chess Federation (FIDE), announces that, further to the Company's announcement of 12 February 2026, Jamison F. Firestone, currently Senior Independent Director, has been appointed Interim Chair of the Board with immediate effect.
Mr Firestone will serve as Interim Chair while the Board conducts a formal search for a permanent Chair.
About Jamison F. Firestone
Mr Firestone is an internationally recognised attorney and a member of the New York Bar, based in London. He was managing partner of Firestone Duncan a combined legal and audit practice for sixteen years and is the founder of FD Advisory, an international law practice, advising clients on sanctions and cross-border matters.
Over a career spanning more than three decades, Mr Firestone has become a globally respected figure in the fields of international law, corporate governance and the rule of law. He has been a long-standing public advocate for transparency and integrity in business, and his work has been cited and featured in leading international publications, including Foreign Policy, The Lawyer and the Financial Times. He has advised governments and parliamentary bodies in multiple jurisdictions on matters of sanctions and accountability.
Mr Firestone has been a Senior Independent Director at World Chess Plc and brings to the role of Interim Chair significant experience in international corporate governance and the oversight of complex cross-border organisations.
Statement by Jamison F. Firestone, Interim Chair
"It is a privilege to step into this role and help The Board to identify the right permanent Chair to lead the Company through its next phase of growth, and I look forward to supporting the management team in the meantime. World Chess operates at the intersection of sport, media and technology, in a sector that demands the highest standards of governance."
Statement by Ilya Merenzon, CEO
"Jamison's appointment as Interim Chair provides the Board with continuity, independence and considerable international standing at a time when the Company is executing on an ambitious growth strategy. His global stage and reputation is exactly the kind of leadership the Board values."
Chair Succession Process
The Company has commenced a process to appoint a permanent Chair. A further announcement will be made in due course.
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended).
For more information, visit:
www.worldchess.com
or contact:
World Chess
Ilya Merenzon, CEO

+44 (0) 20 7469 0930
About World Chess Plc
World Chess (LSE: CHSS) is a leading chess organisation that develops and operates worldchess.com, a subscription platform with over one million users and the official online platform of the International Chess Federation (FIDE). The Company organized several World Chess Championships, including in New York and London. It organises the Armageddon Series and produces The World Chess Show, distributed across more than 50 international markets. More at worldchess.com.

company_30s.what_they_do: CHSS.L runs a global chess entertainment brand, producing televised chess shows, live events and digital content for fans and sponsors.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Chess is a fast‑growing niche sport with a loyal fan base that attracts advertisers and sponsors. By partnering with tech firms and national federations, the company taps a growing market for esports‑style content and monetises through sponsorships, streaming rights and merchandise.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still chasing profitability. Recent filings show a mix of leadership changes, new partnerships and capital raises, but cash reserves are thin and the company has yet to post sustainable earnings. The shares trade at a steep discount to recent highs, reflecting the market’s lack of confidence.

rns_narrative: The company is busy signing partnerships with tech and chess federations, positioning itself to expand the reach of its flagship show. These moves suggest an attempt to diversify revenue and lock in new audiences.
Leadership churn—chair and director changes—indicates a search for a stable vision, while the AGM and board meetings point to an upcoming strategic review.
Capital‑raising activity (share placements, subscription agreements) signals ongoing cash pressure, but also the possibility of fresh funding if the market responds.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Bleak chatter: the community feels the company is drowning in debt and dilution.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: None – there are no bullish posts, which is a classic contrarian sign.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears warn that the company has almost no cash, is still loss‑making, and will likely dilute shareholders further with future placements. They see no viable business model and advise avoiding the stock.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has stayed uniformly negative; there is no hint of optimism or a reversal in discussion.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no public interest is building, so any news could create a surprise bounce.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming AGM could reveal a new strategic plan or a fresh capital raise that might restore confidence.
A formal announcement of a new partnership with a major tech platform or a national federation could unlock new revenue streams.
If the company releases a positive earnings report or a clear path to profitability, that could trigger a rebound.
A sharp volume spike on a support level would confirm that smart money is stepping in.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden increase in trading volume, a price break above the recent low, or any press release that signals a new funding round or a profitable partnership. These are the signals that would validate the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The shares appear to be in the early stages of a deep crash. The APEX score suggests a tight squeeze, but without a confirmed bottom, the recovery could still be a long wait. Confirmation of a bounce will likely come from a volume‑backed move or a clear positive corporate announcement.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the lack of a clear signal color and the ongoing cash crunch make a decisive bullish stance premature.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company can secure a new partnership or raise capital, the steep discount could allow a 4–6× upside once the stock exits the bottom of the crash. The historical record of deep crashes delivering large rebounds supports this possibility.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s thin cash position, repeated capital raises, and lack of profitability mean that a further decline is still possible. A worst‑case scenario would see the stock fall to a new low with limited upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is high relative to the current price, but downside is limited to the current trading level. The asymmetry is roughly 5:1 if a bounce occurs, but the risk of staying flat or falling further is real.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter only after a clear volume‑backed breakout above the recent low. Use a position size that limits exposure to 1–2% of the portfolio. Set a stop just below the breakout level to protect against a false move.
playbook.if_watching: Monitor the upcoming AGM, earnings release, and any partnership announcement. Watch for a 3‑day volume spike and a price break of 2–3% above the last support.
playbook.if_passing: None – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks identified.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Deep crash, no catalysts yet, but partnerships and a potential capital raise could trigger a mean‑reversion bounce, offering high upside with a limited downside.
